req.title: Wire transfer (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall initiate a wire transfer requiring two-factor authentication; transfers above $100,000 require manual review. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.
preconditions: 2fa verified in last 5 min

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-056 defines a wire-transfer endpoint with four distinct outcome classes: (1) normal queued transfer, (2) manual-review path for amounts >$100,000, (3) 401 when 2FA is stale, and (4) error states for insufficient funds, unauthorised access, and exceeded limits. Equivalence Partitioning drives the happy-path (valid amount, fresh 2FA) and the stale-2FA negative partition. Boundary Value Analysis targets the $100,000 threshold with values at $99,999.99, $100,000.00, and $100,000.01. State Transition Coverage captures the 2FA-fresh→stale state flip that changes a 200/202 into a 401. Decision Table cross-products the 2FA-state × amount-tier dimensions. Negative Path covers insufficient-funds and exceeded-limit rejections. All tests are self-contained via fixtures that reset state before each run.

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_standard_amount_fresh_2fa_queued
preconditions: Customer account has sufficient funds (>=10000 USD)
2FA was verified within the last 5 minutes
Transfer amount is within daily limit and below $100,000
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=$5000, currency=USD, valid 2FA session token
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-056: wire queued; compliance check started
R-FIN-056: precondition – 2FA verified in last 5 min
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_wire_transfer_standard_amount_fresh_2fa_queued(
    api_client,          # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session
    fresh_2fa_headers,   # <fixture> returns headers with valid 2FA token (<5 min old)
    funded_account,      # <fixture> ensures account balance >= 10000 USD
):
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "destination_account": "<test_destination_account>",
        "reference": "pytest-R-FIN-056-positive"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=fresh_2fa_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body, "Missing transfer_id in response"
    assert body.get("status") == "queued", f"Unexpected status: {body.get('status')}"
    assert body.get("compliance_check") == "started", (
        f"Unexpected compliance_check: {body.get('compliance_check')}"
    )

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_stale_2fa_returns_401
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient funds
2FA session token is older than 5 minutes (stale state)
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=$5000 and stale 2FA session token
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response error code indicates stale or expired 2FA
trace: R-FIN-056: state transition fresh-2FA → stale-2FA triggers 401
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_wire_transfer_stale_2fa_returns_401(
    api_client,         # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session
    stale_2fa_headers,  # <fixture> returns headers with 2FA token > 5 min old
    funded_account,     # <fixture> ensures account balance >= 10000 USD
):
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "destination_account": "<test_destination_account>",
        "reference": "pytest-R-FIN-056-stale-2fa"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=stale_2fa_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_code = body.get("error_code", "")
    assert "2fa" in error_code.lower() or "authentication" in error_code.lower(), (
        f"Expected 2FA-related error_code, got: {error_code}"
    )
    assert "transfer_id" not in body, "transfer_id must not be present on 401"

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_at_100000_boundary_triggers_manual_review
preconditions: Customer account has sufficient funds (>=100000 USD)
2FA was verified within the last 5 minutes
Amount is exactly $100,000.00 (the boundary value)
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=100000.00, currency=USD, valid 2FA session token
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-056: transfers above $100,000 require manual review
R-FIN-056: error pathway – 202 when manual review required
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_wire_transfer_at_100000_boundary_triggers_manual_review(
    api_client,           # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session
    fresh_2fa_headers,    # <fixture> returns headers with valid 2FA token (<5 min old)
    high_balance_account, # <fixture> ensures account balance >= 100000 USD
):
    payload = {
        "amount": 100000.00,
        "currency": "USD",
        "destination_account": "<test_destination_account>",
        "reference": "pytest-R-FIN-056-boundary-100k"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=fresh_2fa_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body, "Missing transfer_id in 202 response"
    assert body.get("status") == "pending_review", (
        f"Expected 'pending_review', got: {body.get('status')}"
    )
    assert body.get("manual_review") is True, (
        f"Expected manual_review=true, got: {body.get('manual_review')}"
    )

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_below_100000_boundary_not_manual_review
preconditions: Customer account has sufficient funds (>=99999.99 USD)
2FA was verified within the last 5 minutes
Amount is $99,999.99 (one cent below the manual-review threshold)
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=99999.99, currency=USD, valid 2FA session token
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body manual_review is false or absent
trace: R-FIN-056: transfers above $100,000 require manual review
BVA: one unit below boundary ($99,999.99) must not trigger manual review
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_wire_transfer_below_100000_boundary_not_manual_review(
    api_client,           # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session
    fresh_2fa_headers,    # <fixture> returns headers with valid 2FA token (<5 min old)
    high_balance_account, # <fixture> ensures account balance >= 99999.99 USD
):
    payload = {
        "amount": 99999.99,
        "currency": "USD",
        "destination_account": "<test_destination_account>",
        "reference": "pytest-R-FIN-056-boundary-below-100k"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=fresh_2fa_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("status") == "queued", f"Expected 'queued', got: {body.get('status')}"
    assert body.get("manual_review") in (False, None), (
        f"manual_review should be false/absent below threshold, got: {body.get('manual_review')}"
    )

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_insufficient_funds_rejected
preconditions: Customer account balance is less than the requested transfer amount
2FA was verified within the last 5 minutes
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount exceeding account balance, valid 2FA session token
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: NEG: request that violates business rule must be rejected cleanly
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_wire_transfer_insufficient_funds_rejected(
    api_client,            # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session
    fresh_2fa_headers,     # <fixture> returns headers with valid 2FA token (<5 min old)
    low_balance_account,   # <fixture> sets account balance to $1.00
):
    payload = {
        "amount": 50000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "destination_account": "<test_destination_account>",
        "reference": "pytest-R-FIN-056-insufficient-funds"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=fresh_2fa_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (400, 422), (
        f"Expected 400 or 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    error_code = body.get("error_code", "").lower()
    assert "insufficient" in error_code or "funds" in error_code, (
        f"Expected insufficient_funds error_code, got: {error_code}"
    )
    assert "transfer_id" not in body, "transfer_id must not be present on rejection"

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_exceeded_daily_limit_rejected
preconditions: Customer account has sufficient balance
Customer has already consumed the full daily wire transfer limit
2FA was verified within the last 5 minutes
actions: Exhaust the customer daily limit via <exhaust_daily_limit_helper>
POST /transfers/wire with any positive amount using valid 2FA session token
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response error_code indicates limit_exceeded or daily_limit_exceeded
trace: DT: combination of valid 2FA AND sufficient funds AND exceeded-limit → reject
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_wire_transfer_exceeded_daily_limit_rejected(
    api_client,              # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session
    fresh_2fa_headers,       # <fixture> returns headers with valid 2FA token (<5 min old)
    funded_account,          # <fixture> ensures account balance >= 10000 USD
    exhaust_daily_limit,     # <fixture/helper> drives daily limit to zero for this customer
):
    # daily limit already consumed by fixture
    payload = {
        "amount": 100,
        "currency": "USD",
        "destination_account": "<test_destination_account>",
        "reference": "pytest-R-FIN-056-exceeded-limit"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=fresh_2fa_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (422, 429), (
        f"Expected 422 or 429, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    error_code = body.get("error_code", "").lower()
    assert "limit" in error_code, (
        f"Expected limit-related error_code, got: {error_code}"
    )
    assert "transfer_id" not in body, "transfer_id must not be present on limit rejection"

tests.name: test_wire_transfer_above_100000_fresh_2fa_returns_202_manual_review
preconditions: Customer account has sufficient funds (>=200000 USD)
2FA was verified within the last 5 minutes
Transfer amount is $150,000 (clearly above $100,000 threshold)
actions: POST /transfers/wire with amount=150000, currency=USD, valid 2FA session token
Capture HTTP response status and body
trace: R-FIN-056: transfers above $100,000 require manual review
R-FIN-056: error pathway – 202 when manual review required
DT: valid 2FA AND amount>$100k → 202 + manual review
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_wire_transfer_above_100000_fresh_2fa_returns_202_manual_review(
    api_client,           # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session
    fresh_2fa_headers,    # <fixture> returns headers with valid 2FA token (<5 min old)
    high_balance_account, # <fixture> ensures account balance >= 200000 USD
):
    payload = {
        "amount": 150000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "destination_account": "<test_destination_account>",
        "reference": "pytest-R-FIN-056-above-100k-manual"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/transfers/wire",
        json=payload,
        headers=fresh_2fa_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body, "Missing transfer_id in 202 response"
    assert body.get("status") == "pending_review", (
        f"Expected 'pending_review', got: {body.get('status')}"
    )
    assert body.get("compliance_check") == "started", (
        f"Expected compliance_check='started', got: {body.get('compliance_check')}"
    )
